modules.title: #41 Solid red oak strip flooring in a grand Solana Beach estate on the edge of Rancho Santa Fe, carefully restored to its natural color. The first photo shows the floor sanded down to raw wood mid-process, with dust-control plastic protecting sensitive areas and fillers applied between boards. The second photo reveals the finished floor still wet with a mirror-like sheen, hallway lights reflecting across the surface. This long North County San Diego hallway demonstrates traditional hardwood floor refinishing done to perfection, restoring the warmth and beauty of natural red oak.

modules.title: 42 Solid teak parquet flooring in San Diego, a rare mid-century installation with a gorgeous pattern that had aged for decades. The first photo shows the parquet in worn, dull condition before sanding. The second photo captures the transformation after sanding and sealing—rotary equipment and full dust containment brought the teak back to life, revealing deep, rich tones enhanced with an oil sealer and finished to a satin sheen. The intricate parquet design now glows with warmth and precision craftsmanship.

modules.title: #43 Wide plank French oak flooring installation in a stunning La Jolla home above La Jolla Shores on Scenic Drive. This glued-down, unfinished square-edge rustic grade oak was carefully installed in random lengths and sanded to perfection. The first photo shows the raw, freshly installed planks before finishing. The second photo displays the natural beauty of the French oak after sanding, protected with a commercial-grade finish that will last for decades. At 5/8” thick with a 7” width and 4mm top layer, this seamless floor—with no beveled edges or wire brushing—highlights the straight lines, perfect board selection, and coastal craftsmanship of a high-end La Jolla estate overlooking the Pacific.

images.alt: La Jolla hardwood floor installation project above La Jolla Shores, wide plank French oak flooring glued down in random lengths, unfinished rustic grade square edge planks sanded perfectly smooth before finishing, seamless luxury installation by San Diego flooring contractor.

modules.title: #47 Engineered maple hardwood floor refinishing in a 1960s home located between Del Mar and Solana Beach, on the south side of Rancho Santa Fe. These maple floors had yellowed, scratched, and sun-faded over decades, with a medallion centerpiece in the entryway, and the owners were ready to replace them. Instead, we restored them to perfection—repairing water damage from leaky windows, sanding the surface smooth, and finishing with a natural color and matte sheen that brought back the true beauty of the maple. The transformation surprised the homeowners, who decided to keep their original floors. This North County San Diego project highlights our specialty in repairing, refinishing, and custom finishing engineered maple floors while keeping homes clean and dust-free.

images.alt: Engineered maple hardwood floor refinishing Del Mar Solana Beach Rancho Santa Fe area, 1960s home with scratched and sun-faded maple repaired and sanded smooth, water damage from windows fixed, floors finished with natural color matte sheen, North County San Diego contractor restoring coastal homes.

The result is a flawless parquet surface that highlights the natural beauty of walnut, showing how even intricate patterned floors can be renewed and protected for decades of use in coastal San Diego homes.

modules.title: #49 Solid wide plank rift and quartered white oak floor installation in Pacific Beach, San Diego. This select-grade, 4-inch random length flooring was nailed down in the traditional method and site finished for a seamless natural look. Using full dust-containment sanding equipment, the planks were sanded and coated cleanly in-home without airborne debris. Rift and quartered oak provides unmatched stability and a refined grain pattern, making this floor both durable and elegant for coastal San Diego living. Installed and finished by a licensed flooring contractor specializing in traditional solid hardwood floors.

modules.title: #50 Solid 3-inch wide rift and quartered white oak hardwood flooring installation in San Diego. This project began with plywood installation, followed by nail-down installation of unfinished select-grade oak. Once installed, the narrow planks were site finished to perfection with a Bona stain and protected with Bona commercial-grade polyurethane for lasting durability. The rift and quartered cut creates straight, elegant grain lines and excellent stability, giving this home a timeless look. Completed with dust-containment sanding equipment, this project showcases traditional craftsmanship and flawless finishing by a licensed San Diego hardwood floor contractor.
